content.text: in Claude's system is the process of activating the Citations API feature so that Claude automatically generates precise source references linking its claims back to exact sentences and passages in uploaded documents.
to your API requests and marking source documents with citation settings, so Claude automatically includes references like
"When Claude answers questions based on documents you provide (PDFs or plain text), it doesn't just generate fluent responses — it shows exactly where each claim comes from."
Every claim cited to source text

"Previously, developers relied on complex prompts that instruct Claude to include source information... With Citations, users can now add source documents to the context window, and when querying the model, Claude automatically cites claims in its output."
Step 2: Enable Citations in Document Block

# Citations appear in response metadata
print(response.citations)  # List of citation objects

# Each citation includes:
for citation in response.citations:
    print(f"Cited text: {citation.cited_text}")
    print(f"Document: {citation.document_title}")
    print(f"Pages: {citation.start_page_number}-{citation.end_page_number}")
    print(f"Chars: {citation.start_char}-{citation.end_char}")
    print(f"Document index: {citation.document_index}")
Upload multiple documents with citations enabled:
"Summary: Atmosphere formed from volcanic gases [p. 15]" + "View Source" button
- Every claim verifiable to source document
